Operation 'inputAuthenticationFlow' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description is too vague about what inputs are expected and what the response contains. Added clarity about flow state identifier, types of input data, return value structure, and state modification side effect for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Provide input to an authentication flow
data.current_description: Advances an authentication flow by providing the required input for the current step.
data.description_suggestion: Advances an authentication flow by providing the required input for the current step. Accepts flow state identifier and step-specific input data (e.g., credentials, OTP, passkey response). Returns the updated AuthenticationFlowResponse containing the next step or completion status. Modifies the flow state on the server.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about what inputs are expected and what the response contains. Added clarity about flow state identifier, types of input data, return value structure, and state modification side effect for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'getAuthenticationFlowState' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description lacks specificity about required inputs (flow state identifier) and response structure (AuthenticationFlowResponse). Added clarity about what must be provided and what will be returned to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Get current state of an authentication flow
data.current_description: Retrieves the current state of an in-progress authentication flow.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current state of an in-progress authentication flow. Requires the flow state identifier as input. Returns an AuthenticationFlowResponse containing the current step, required inputs, and flow metadata. Does not modify flow state.
data.reasoning: Description lacks specificity about required inputs (flow state identifier) and response structure (AuthenticationFlowResponse). Added clarity about what must be provided and what will be returned to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'websocketAuthenticationFlow' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Summary is too generic and doesn't convey the real-time subscription nature. Description lacks input parameter details and response structure information. Enhanced summary to emphasize subscription behavior and added clarity about required parameters, response format, and use cases for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: WebSocket endpoint that delivers real-time updates when the authentication flow state changes (e.g. when the user finishes OAuth or passkey flow in another window).
data.summary_suggestion: Subscribe to real-time authentication flow state updates
data.description_suggestion: WebSocket endpoint that delivers real-time updates when the authentication flow state changes. Requires flow state identifier as a query parameter. Maintains an open connection and pushes AuthenticationFlowResponse updates when the flow progresses (e.g., when the user completes OAuth or passkey authentication in another window). Useful for multi-window authentication scenarios.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic and doesn't convey the real-time subscription nature. Description lacks input parameter details and response structure information. Enhanced summary to emphasize subscription behavior and added clarity about required parameters, response format, and use cases for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'oauthToken' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is incomplete about input variations and response structure. Added clarity about supported grant types, required parameters for each flow, and detailed response fields (TokenResponse schema) to help AI agents understand when and how to use this endpoint.

data.current_description: Exchange authorization code for access token, or refresh an access token.
data.description_suggestion: OAuth 2.0 token endpoint that exchanges authorization codes for access tokens or refreshes expired access tokens. Accepts either authorization_code grant (with code and redirect_uri) or refresh_token grant (with refresh_token). Returns TokenResponse containing access_token, token_type, expires_in, and optionally refresh_token and id_token.
data.reasoning: Description is incomplete about input variations and response structure. Added clarity about supported grant types, required parameters for each flow, and detailed response fields (TokenResponse schema) to help AI agents understand when and how to use this endpoint.

message: Operation 'oauthRevoke' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description lacks input parameter details and side effect information. Added clarity about required parameters, success response, and the critical side effect that revoked tokens become invalid. This helps AI agents understand the operation's impact and when to use it appropriately.

data.current_description: Revokes an access token or refresh token.
data.description_suggestion: Revokes an access token or refresh token, invalidating it immediately. Accepts token and token_type_hint parameters. Returns HTTP 200 on success. Revoked tokens can no longer be used for API requests or token refresh operations. Useful for logout and security scenarios.
data.reasoning: Description lacks input parameter details and side effect information. Added clarity about required parameters, success response, and the critical side effect that revoked tokens become invalid. This helps AI agents understand the operation's impact and when to use it appropriately.

data.current_description: Returns claims about the authenticated user.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve authenticated user information and claims
data.description_suggestion: Returns OpenID Connect user information claims for the authenticated user. Requires a valid OAuth 2.0 access token via Bearer authentication. Returns a UserInfo object containing user identity claims such as subject identifier, name, email, and other profile information.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too technical/endpoint-focused rather than action-oriented. data.current_description: Returns the OpenID Connect discovery document.
data.description_suggestion: Returns the OpenID Connect discovery document containing server metadata and endpoint information. This document includes URLs for authorization, token, userinfo, and JWKS endpoints, as well as supported authentication methods, response types, and other server capabilities. No authentication required.
data.reasoning: Original summary is endpoint-focused rather than action-oriented. data.current_description: Returns the JSON Web Key Set used to sign tokens.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve JSON Web Key Set for token verification
data.description_suggestion: Returns the JSON Web Key Set (JWKS) containing the public keys used to verify the signatures of ID tokens and access tokens issued by this server. Clients use these keys to validate token authenticity. No authentication required. The JWKS is typically cached by clients.
data.reasoning: Original summary is endpoint-focused rather than action-oriented. Description lacks context about the purpose (token verification) and use case. Enhanced version explains why clients need this endpoint, how the keys are used, and clarifies public accessibility for better AI agent understanding of when to call this operation.

data.current_description: Initiates a bulk user import. Accepts a JSON body with an array of user records. Returns a task ID for tracking progress.
data.description_suggestion: Initiates a bulk user import operation by accepting a JSON array of UserImportRecord objects. Requires admin API authentication. Returns a task ID that can be used to poll the import status via the getUserImportStatus endpoint. The import operation is asynchronous and processes users in the background. Imported users are created in the system with their provided credentials and attributes.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate and action-oriented. Description needs enhancement to clarify: authentication requirement (admin API), the schema of input records (UserImportRecord), the asynchronous nature of the operation, how to track progress, and the side effect (users are created in the system). These details are critical for AI agents to understand when and how to use this operation.
